name: butler
description: Executes blueprints. /butler:dispatch-fleet starts a deterministic, token-free orchestrator script (no Claude) that watches, launches, relaunches, and usage-governs detached headless coordinator sessions (one per package, scoped worker subagents, hook-enforced discipline: ledger freshness, write-set containment, single-writer, git safety) and auto-resumes across usage/token limits; /butler:drive-solo is the single interactive execute verb — a thin loop over the perl director bp-drive-next.pl that drives one blueprint, a named set, or ALL audited blueprints to done in one host-or-sandbox session, with a flat worker layer, director-managed keep-awake and self-governed usage-limit sleep-through; /butler:reporter is the interactive front door; /butler:status reports. Start-or-continue (no resume verb). The fleet is sandbox-only — pairs with the host-side `blueprint` plugin that authors the blueprints it runs.
